channel.title: Junkyard Dogs
channel.itunes:author: Joe Skipper
channel.itunes:subtitle: Joe Skipper's thoughts on what's happening in triathlon
channel.itunes:summary:
This is a podcast talking about all things triathlon and endurance, with a humorous twist.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
channel.description:
This is a podcast talking about all things triathlon and endurance, with a humorous twist.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Jake Dearden Hyrox Elite 15 and 2.22 Marathon
item.itunes:title: Jake Dearden Hyrox Elite 15 and 2.22 Marathon
item.description:
On this episode i speak to Hyrox elite 15 athlete Jake dearden. Jake has managed to become one of the best hyrox athletes in the world and as well as being an elite 15 athlete he ran 2.28 at the Berlin marathon in 2024 and then went on to run 2.22 at the Houston marathon. This episode was just days before Jake attempted a sub 2.20 marathon at Houston. It’s great to see what jakes attitude and mindset is like and what makes him tick.



Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
On this episode i speak to Hyrox elite 15 athlete Jake dearden. Jake has managed to become one of the best hyrox athletes in the world and as well as being an elite 15 athlete he ran 2.28 at the Berlin marathon in 2024 and then went on to run 2.22 at the Houston marathon. This episode was just days before Jake attempted a sub 2.20 marathon at Houston. It’s great to see what jakes attitude and mindset is like and what makes him tick.



Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Talking sport end of year wrap up
item.itunes:title: Talking sport end of year wrap up
item.description:
Me and Josh have a chat about our best sporting moments from the year and talk about our predictions for next year and what we would like to see happen.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
Me and Josh have a chat about our best sporting moments from the year and talk about our predictions for next year and what we would like to see happen.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Phil Sesemann - "How I ran a 2:07 Marathon"
item.itunes:title: JYD - Phil Sesemann - "How I ran a 2:07 Marathon"
item.description:
Joe sits down with British marathon star Phil Sesemann fresh off an unreal autumn double, running 2:07 in Amsterdam and backing it up with another 2:07 in Valencia just weeks later. Phil breaks down why Amsterdam felt like a dream day while Valencia became a grind, how small differences in weather and pacing can turn a marathon into a completely different race, and what it is really like in those final 10 kilometres when the legs start negotiating.


The conversation goes right back to Phil’s origins, from swimming and early cross country success, to a year inside the Leeds triathlon setup, before committing fully to running after clocking 14:27 for 5K during that period. Phil explains why he was a 1500m runner for years, how COVID-era long runs helped unlock his marathon potential, and how his debut at London (2:12:58) set him on the path that eventually led to Olympic qualification.


Phil also opens up about the obsession behind the progress: a “perfect week” of marathon training featuring double thresholds, marathon-specific sessions, big steady runs, and mileage that can hit around 150 miles per week. He talks honestly about mistakes he has made with session frequency, why the best sessions can fool you into getting greedy, and how he tries to stay on the right side of the line between fitness and breakdown.


There is plenty of gold on recovery and fueling too, including Phil’s simple non-negotiables: sleep, eating enough, and his famously consistent post-run routine of a pint of whole milk Nesquik after every run. He shares how he thinks about staying healthy, why he believes fueling enables training rather than simply supporting it, and how consistency over years is the real superpower.


The chat finishes with Phil reflecting on the Paris Olympics experience, what he would do differently next time, the pride of hitting the Olympic standard by seconds, and the mindset traits he believes separate athletes who make it from those who fade out. Plus, a fun closer on his toughest rival battles and the satisfaction of finally getting one back when it mattered most.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Phil at https://www.instagram.com/philsesemann/

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
Joe sits down with British marathon star Phil Sesemann fresh off an unreal autumn double, running 2:07 in Amsterdam and backing it up with another 2:07 in Valencia just weeks later. Phil breaks down why Amsterdam felt like a dream day while Valencia became a grind, how small differences in weather and pacing can turn a marathon into a completely different race, and what it is really like in those final 10 kilometres when the legs start negotiating.


The conversation goes right back to Phil’s origins, from swimming and early cross country success, to a year inside the Leeds triathlon setup, before committing fully to running after clocking 14:27 for 5K during that period. Phil explains why he was a 1500m runner for years, how COVID-era long runs helped unlock his marathon potential, and how his debut at London (2:12:58) set him on the path that eventually led to Olympic qualification.


Phil also opens up about the obsession behind the progress: a “perfect week” of marathon training featuring double thresholds, marathon-specific sessions, big steady runs, and mileage that can hit around 150 miles per week. He talks honestly about mistakes he has made with session frequency, why the best sessions can fool you into getting greedy, and how he tries to stay on the right side of the line between fitness and breakdown.


There is plenty of gold on recovery and fueling too, including Phil’s simple non-negotiables: sleep, eating enough, and his famously consistent post-run routine of a pint of whole milk Nesquik after every run. He shares how he thinks about staying healthy, why he believes fueling enables training rather than simply supporting it, and how consistency over years is the real superpower.


The chat finishes with Phil reflecting on the Paris Olympics experience, what he would do differently next time, the pride of hitting the Olympic standard by seconds, and the mindset traits he believes separate athletes who make it from those who fade out. Plus, a fun closer on his toughest rival battles and the satisfaction of finally getting one back when it mattered most.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD

item.title: JYD - Jimmy Whelan
item.itunes:title: JYD - Jimmy Whelan
item.description:
Joe is joined by former WorldTour pro and freshly minted 61-minute half marathoner Jimmy Whelan for a cracking deep dive into life on the limit. Jimmy talks through how he went from a talented young runner to winning the under-23 Tour of Flanders and racing Grand Tours with EF, what it is really like bombing down wet descents at 70 kilometres an hour, and why getting repeatedly taken out by crashes finally pushed him away from pro cycling.


He breaks down the “devil block” weeks that built his monster engine, the difference between pure watts and fatigue resistance, and what it feels like when riders like Mathieu van der Poel and Julian Alaphilippe simply ride away from you. Then Jimmy explains how a sciatica flare-up on the TT bike nudged him back into running, the four-month build to his debut 70.3 in Geelong, and the big block that ended with a 61.30 half in Valencia where he went through 10K in 28.39 and ran with Alex Yee and the British marathon crew.


Jimmy finishes by opening up on his next chapter as a full-time runner, his three-year plan toward the LA Olympics, and whether triathlon is completely off the table or still quietly in the mix.

Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Jimmy at https://www.instagram.com/jimmywhelann

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Joe is joined by former WorldTour pro and freshly minted 61-minute half marathoner Jimmy Whelan for a cracking deep dive into life on the limit. Jimmy talks through how he went from a talented young runner to winning the under-23 Tour of Flanders and racing Grand Tours with EF, what it is really like bombing down wet descents at 70 kilometres an hour, and why getting repeatedly taken out by crashes finally pushed him away from pro cycling.


He breaks down the “devil block” weeks that built his monster engine, the difference between pure watts and fatigue resistance, and what it feels like when riders like Mathieu van der Poel and Julian Alaphilippe simply ride away from you. Then Jimmy explains how a sciatica flare-up on the TT bike nudged him back into running, the four-month build to his debut 70.3 in Geelong, and the big block that ended with a 61.30 half in Valencia where he went through 10K in 28.39 and ran with Alex Yee and the British marathon crew.


Jimmy finishes by opening up on his next chapter as a full-time runner, his three-year plan toward the LA Olympics, and whether triathlon is completely off the table or still quietly in the mix.

item.title: JYD - How We Train Episode 5
item.itunes:title: JYD - How We Train Episode 5
item.description:
Joe and Tyler are back for another episode of Junk Yard Dogs. Their last show was recorded together in person, but this time they are checking in from Arizona during the build up to Ironman Arizona. Joe is racing and Tyler is on support duty, fresh off one of the wildest three week racing stretches of his career.


Tyler talks through everything that happened at Langkawi 70.3, starting with waking up feverish, losing power and aircon before the race, crashing on slippery roads while dodging a stray dog, riding completely alone for most of the bike and still running himself onto the podium in brutal heat. A week later he lines up for a World Cup in Japan with no voice and a wrecked body but still manages to swim near the front thanks to smart race awareness and open water craft.


From there they dive into what is shaping the front of the sport right now. They break down the Blummenfelt versus Jelle battle at 70.3 Worlds, how depth and aerodynamics have changed racing, and whether a bigger draft zone would actually fix the pack problem. They get into training alone versus training with a squad, why some pros avoid training with each other, how much rest runners and triathletes really need in hard sessions, and how Joe would structure training if an age grouper only had ten hours a week.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
Joe and Tyler are back for another episode of Junk Yard Dogs. Their last show was recorded together in person, but this time they are checking in from Arizona during the build up to Ironman Arizona. Joe is racing and Tyler is on support duty, fresh off one of the wildest three week racing stretches of his career.


Tyler talks through everything that happened at Langkawi 70.3, starting with waking up feverish, losing power and aircon before the race, crashing on slippery roads while dodging a stray dog, riding completely alone for most of the bike and still running himself onto the podium in brutal heat. A week later he lines up for a World Cup in Japan with no voice and a wrecked body but still manages to swim near the front thanks to smart race awareness and open water craft.


From there they dive into what is shaping the front of the sport right now. They break down the Blummenfelt versus Jelle battle at 70.3 Worlds, how depth and aerodynamics have changed racing, and whether a bigger draft zone would actually fix the pack problem. They get into training alone versus training with a squad, why some pros avoid training with each other, how much rest runners and triathletes really need in hard sessions, and how Joe would structure training if an age grouper only had ten hours a week.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - How We Train Episode 4
item.itunes:title: JYD - How We Train Episode 4
item.itunes:subtitle: Pre-Ironman Arizona
item.description:
Joe is in Tempe getting ready to race Ironman Arizona, Tyler is on the ground as a very enthusiastic spectator, and for the first time ever they are recording together in person.


In this episode, the boys give a proper pre-race low down on the final edition of Ironman Arizona, from the weird swim cancellation rules and Tempe Town Lake water testing, to who is actually favourite on paper and how Joe plans to race it. They get into how he wants to start the swim, why he is eyeing “Terminator” Rasmus Svenningsson as his golden ticket to the front, how not to overbike if you want to run under 2.40, and what result would leave Joe happy, content, or seriously disappointed. There are plenty of stories along the way, including Joe rocking up drenched to the group run, recognising half the pro field on the start line in real life for the first time, getting ghosted by Trovo Foley on a “first date” swim, and Tyler explaining why some athletes never want to train with anyone during race week.

Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Paula Findlay
item.itunes:title: JYD - Paula Findlay
item.description:
Canadian star Paula Findlay joins Joe for a wide open chat that runs from her meteoric WTCS rise and the weight of London 2012 to the joy of finding her groove in middle distance racing. Paula talks about switching off the gadgets to actually feel her body, why Strava can mess with your head, and how meeting Eric Lagerstrom reshaped her approach to triathlon as a true small business. She breaks down the difference between time trial power and tri power, winning St George years apart in a faster era, and why Holly Lawrence brings the best out of her on race day. There is honest reflection on federation support when you are hot and when you are not, identity beyond results, and how COVID unexpectedly became a reset that led to Challenge Daytona glory and the birth of TTL. Paula shares Ironman plans on the horizon with an eye on Taupō, a playful case for downhill skiing as a fourth discipline, and the simple pleasure of coffee, chocolate, and quiet dog runs. Joe adds a cautionary tale about jalapeños before race day, plus a pitch for go-karts to fix bike descending. A candid, uplifting listen with plenty for pros and age-groupers alike.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe


You can follow Paula at https://www.instagram.com/paula_findlay

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Paula shares Ironman plans on the horizon with an eye on Taupō, a playful case for downhill skiing as a fourth discipline, and the simple pleasure of coffee, chocolate, and quiet dog runs. item.title: JYD - How We Train Episode 3
item.itunes:title: JYD - How We Train Episode 3
item.description:
Joe and Tyler reunite for an unfiltered catch up that runs from altitude life in Flagstaff to a three race swing through Australia, Malaysia and Japan. Joe revisits his chaotic first Ironman in 2012 with a two gel and peanut butter sandwich plan, and both laugh at the early days of jelly babies and Snickers on the top tube. Tyler talks through a recent crash, the grind of late season form, and the constant battle to keep weight on, while Joe recounts a family holiday in Turkey that turned into water slide warfare. They finish with a bold preview of the Kona women’s race, weighing Lucy Charles Barclay, Taylor Knibb, Kat Matthews and Laura Philipp, then lock in a friendly wager that ends with a beer mile in Arizona. Candid chat, useful numbers, and plenty of pro level banter.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can folllw Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Joe revisits his chaotic first Ironman in 2012 with a two gel and peanut butter sandwich plan, and both laugh at the early days of jelly babies and Snickers on the top tube. Tyler talks through a recent crash, the grind of late season form, and the constant battle to keep weight on, while Joe recounts a family holiday in Turkey that turned into water slide warfare. They finish with a bold preview of the Kona women’s race, weighing Lucy Charles Barclay, Taylor Knibb, Kat Matthews and Laura Philipp, then lock in a friendly wager that ends with a beer mile in Arizona. item.title: JYD - How We Train Episode 2
item.itunes:title: JYD - How We Train Episode 2
item.description:
Joe catches up with Canada’s Tyler Mislawchuk after his blistering Super League run in Chicago. They unpack how team tactics shape Super League racing, why the swims get slower but harder, and how Tyler’s longer aerobic work is boosting both Super League and WTCS form. The chat veers into friendly ribbing about hairlines, weekend race plans, and then pivots to Joe’s Copenhagen tale of a great ride undone by a puncture and why he is now all-in on Nice. They trade views on descent hype at Nice, Morgan Pearson’s antics, and the steady drumbeat of doping news. The second half turns into a fun coaching thought experiment where Joe maps a Kona plan for Tyler and Tyler returns the favour with heat-prep, swim technique tweaks, and hill sprints. They wrap with favourite sessions from the last few weeks.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe


You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - How We Train Episode 1
item.itunes:title: JYD - How We Train Episode 1
item.description:
Joe Skipper is back and joined by Tyler Mislawchuk for the start of a new JYD series.


In this episode, Joe and Tyler dive into what’s been happening in the triathlon world, answer listener questions, and chat all things training, altitude, brick sessions, zone two riding, and knowing when to back off.


They talk openly about injuries, coaching versus self-coaching, and how to balance racing between Super League and 70.3s. Tyler shares what it’s like training at 2,100 metres in Flagstaff, reflects on his Olympic journey, and breaks down what really happened in that near photo finish with Alex Yee.


This episode covers everything from run drills and barefoot cooldowns to VO2 max myths, carbon shoes, and whether Jakob Ingebrigtsen is pushing his luck coming back from injury.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
Joe Skipper is back and joined by Tyler Mislawchuk for the start of a new JYD series.


In this episode, Joe and Tyler dive into what’s been happening in the triathlon world, answer listener questions, and chat all things training, altitude, brick sessions, zone two riding, and knowing when to back off.


They talk openly about injuries, coaching versus self-coaching, and how to balance racing between Super League and 70.3s. Tyler shares what it’s like training at 2,100 metres in Flagstaff, reflects on his Olympic journey, and breaks down what really happened in that near photo finish with Alex Yee.


This episode covers everything from run drills and barefoot cooldowns to VO2 max myths, carbon shoes, and whether Jakob Ingebrigtsen is pushing his luck coming back from injury.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Frankfurt and Roth
item.itunes:title: JYD - Frankfurt and Roth
item.description:
Joe and Tom catch up after the Ironman European Championship in Frankfurt, breaking down the race and sharing their own recent experiences.


Tom reflects on missing his race due to car troubles despite being in top form, while Joe talks about his own training and upcoming races. They dive into the performances in Frankfurt, including Kristian Blummenfelt’s smart racing, Kristian Hogenhaug’s strong showing before a controversial penalty, and how tactics played out on the bike and run. The conversation also covers the chaotic officiating that impacted the race outcome, with both Joe and Tom criticising inconsistent and heavy-handed referee decisions.


They discuss the recent change of venue for the T100 series, with Tom highlighting the double standards in how race organisers portray different countries. The episode wraps up with a look ahead to Challenge Roth and some light-hearted chat about travel, training and triathlon culture.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER 


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tom at https://www.instagram.com/tom_epton_

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
item.itunes:summary:
Joe and Tom catch up after the Ironman European Championship in Frankfurt, breaking down the race and sharing their own recent experiences.


Tom reflects on missing his race due to car troubles despite being in top form, while Joe talks about his own training and upcoming races. They dive into the performances in Frankfurt, including Kristian Blummenfelt’s smart racing, Kristian Hogenhaug’s strong showing before a controversial penalty, and how tactics played out on the bike and run. The conversation also covers the chaotic officiating that impacted the race outcome, with both Joe and Tom criticising inconsistent and heavy-handed referee decisions.


They discuss the recent change of venue for the T100 series, with Tom highlighting the double standards in how race organisers portray different countries. The episode wraps up with a look ahead to Challenge Roth and some light-hearted chat about travel, training and triathlon culture.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Ironman Austria Debrief
item.itunes:title: JYD - Ironman Austria Debrief
item.description:
In this honest and wide-ranging episode of the JYD Podcast, Joe Skipper opens up about his tough day at Ironman Austria. Despite solid training and a good taper, Joe felt completely empty on the bike and run. He breaks down the race from a decent non-wetsuit swim to a sluggish ride and a run that never got going. He suspects illness or the effects of altitude might be to blame and shares the frustration of doing everything right but still having it fall apart on race day.


Joined by regular guest Tom Epton, the conversation turns reflective as they discuss the emotional rollercoaster of professional sport. They talk about how rare the highs are, how common setbacks feel, and the challenge of staying motivated through it all. Joe talks about trying to reset and look ahead to Challenge Roth as a shot at redemption.


The episode also covers recent pro racing including:


Ironman Cairns where Matt Marquardt impressed with a comeback win after cramping early


70.3 Boulder with a big win for Sam Appleton and rising American Colin Szuch making waves


The T100 series where both Joe and Tom voice strong opinions about the current format. They argue for larger fields, open qualification, and fewer distractions like mid-race interviews


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tom at https://www.instagram.com/tom_epton_


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Despite solid training and a good taper, Joe felt completely empty on the bike and run. He breaks down the race from a decent non-wetsuit swim to a sluggish ride and a run that never got going. He suspects illness or the effects of altitude might be to blame and shares the frustration of doing everything right but still having it fall apart on race day.


Joined by regular guest Tom Epton, the conversation turns reflective as they discuss the emotional rollercoaster of professional sport. item.title: JYD - T100 San Francisco Predictions
item.itunes:title: JYD - T100 San Francisco Predictions
item.description:
Joe’s joined by Tom Epton in Font-Romeau for a classic Junk Yard Dogs chat. They talk training setups, altitude benefits, and the contrast between Andorra and the French mountains. The pair dissect recent race results and take a closer look at the upcoming T100 San Francisco, including a possible repeat showdown between Marten Van Riel, Kyle Smith, and Rico Bogen.


They share candid takes on prize money, the shifting landscape of long-course triathlon, and finish with a chaotic deep-dive into the Enhanced Games and the absurdity of doping in sport. Unfiltered, scrappy, and completely on brand.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER  


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tom at https://www.instagram.com/tom_epton_

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

The pair dissect recent race results and take a closer look at the upcoming T100 San Francisco, including a possible repeat showdown between Marten Van Riel, Kyle Smith, and Rico Bogen.

item.title: JYD - Recent Race Recaps
item.itunes:title: JYD - Recent Race Recaps
item.description:
Joe and Tom catch up from Font-Romeau and Andorra with a full debrief on recent races including the Challenge Championship, Yokohama WTCS, and 70.3 Chattanooga. From Belinda’s commentary masterclass to dodgy tactics and draft fests, they don’t hold back. Plus, Joe relives his brutal Ironman Lanzarote meltdown, and the pair get into a passionate rant about what’s going wrong in pro racing right now.


They also discuss The Enhanced Games on the 2nd half of this week's show available exclusively for JYD Members!


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 

Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tom at https://www.instagram.com/tom_epton_



Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - St George 70.3 Preview
item.itunes:title: JYD - St George 70.3 Preview
item.description:
Joe is joined by Tom Epton to disect all the drama from Ironman Texas - Kat vs Taylor, a dominant Blummenfelt, and whether over biking cost more than just time. They look ahead to St George 70.3, previewing both stacked fields, picking podiums, and debating tactics, swim groups, and who can run their way to glory. Plus, gravel talk, team time trials, questionable life balance in pro sport, and plans for an Olympic-distance showdown in Font-Romeu.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 


Get 20% off your nutrition with Styrkr: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER


To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tom at https://www.instagram.com/tom_epton_

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Jonny Brownlee
item.itunes:title: JYD - Jonny Brownlee
item.description:
Joe catches up with Olympic triathlon legend Jonny Brownlee for a proper chat about a career that has had just about everything. From running to school with a backpack full of books to standing on Olympic podiums alongside his brother Alistair, Jonny reflects on the highs, the tough moments and everything in between.


They talk through the golden years of ITU racing, what it was like training with Team Sky, almost switching to pro cycling, and why Rio 2016 still leaves a bit of a sting. There are plenty of laughs too, including a story about Alistair going out on the beers and then smashing a long ride the next morning like nothing happened.


This one is full of insight, honesty and great stories from one of the sport’s most respected and consistent athletes.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 

Fuel up with Styrkr and save 20%: https://styrkr.com/SKIPPER



To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Jonny at https://www.instagram.com/jonnybrownleetri

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Ben Hoffman
item.itunes:title: JYD - Ben Hoffman
item.description:
This week Joe is joined by American professional triathlete, Ben Hoffman. Ben has finished second in Kona and beat Magnus Ditlev at Ironman Texas in 2022.


They discuss the times they have raced each other and how those races have gone. They also preview this weekend's Ironman Texas.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 



To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Ben at https://www.instagram.com/bhoffmanracing

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: JYD - Tyler Misalwchuk
item.itunes:title: JYD - Tyler Misalwchuk
item.description:
In this episode I sit down with Tyler Mislawchuk, a Canadian triathlete who has raced at three Olympic Games and has one of the most interesting stories in the sport. We chat about how he went from playing ice hockey in freezing cold Winnipeg to racing on the world’s biggest triathlon stages.


Tyler opens up about the tough transition from short course to long course racing and what really happened to him at T100 Singapore. We talk about some of the wild stuff he’s experienced, like learning to swim properly at 16, training through minus 40 winters, being written off by his national federation, and how he made his first Olympic team by going completely off the traditional path.


There’s plenty of banter, some good laughs, and loads of insight into what it takes to make it in this sport. Tyler’s story is all about resilience and I reckon you’ll enjoy this one as much as I did.


Join the JYD for exclusive members perks: patreon.com/TheJYD


Find out more about Valere: tinyurl.com/valerebigdog 



To watch this podcast as a video visit: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC0VKd5PFrhj-qsnWw8VVmyQ



Or check us out on Social Media: 


Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/junk_yard_dogz 

 

You can follow Joe at https://www.instagram.com/notanotheraveragejoe

You can follow Tyler at https://www.instagram.com/therealmislawchuk

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

Tyler opens up about the tough transition from short course to long course racing and what really happened to him at T100 Singapore. We talk about some of the wild stuff he’s experienced, like learning to swim properly at 16, training through minus 40 winters, being written off by his national federation, and how he made his first Olympic team by going completely off the traditional path.
